responses: there was no executive session during the meeting of
January 16, 1996. During the meeting the Presbytery arose as a
Presbytery, was seated as a Corporation, elected officers, arose as a
Corporation and was again seated as a Presbytery. The business
transacted in that meeting is recorded within the minutes of Presbytery, not as separate minutes.

description: Expiration of license to preach. Then licentiate did not apply for renewal. Procedures for renewal not followed.
responses: Presbytery met only two times in Stated Meetings in 1996: in January and again in October. In the interval between the meetings TE Ron Davis of Bethel Presbyterian Church in Lake Charles died. Licentiate RE Ben Thompson, a member of Bethel Presbyterian Church whose license was still valid, was asked by the Session of Bethel Presbyterian Church to fill the pulpit. Before the Stated meeting in October RE Thompson’s license expired. When the Presbytery discovered the oversight in October 1996, the motion was made, citing BCO 19-2, to waive re-examination in this extraordinary case and to renew his license. RE Thompson had been preaching regularly under the supervision of the Bethel Session.

description: Session records not reviewed in 1996.
responses: The Session Records Committee apparently did fail to examine minutes in 1996 due to the departure of the committee members from Presbytery. Subsequently, session records have been reviewed by the committee.

description: No standing rules submitted per RAO 14-4-c&e (second year). Adopted
The minutes of the called meeting do not state the purpose for the meeting. BCO 13-12.
Presbytery did not record why it considers a work a valid Christian ministry. BCO 8-7; 13-5; 20-1.
The report of the commission to install teaching elders contains no record of the following:
The pronouncement of installation (BCO 21-7);
The charge to the congregation (BCO 21-7);
That the TEs answered the constitutional questions in the affirmative (BCO 21-9);
That the congregation or session were asked the constitutional questions (BCO 21-10);
The Standing Rules mandate a meeting in October, yet no minutes for that meeting were submitted;
The Standing Rules are not complete. (RAO 14.4.c.2).
No record of candidate questions put to transferring TE. BCO 13-6.
responses: While presbytery did not specifically ask Rev. Amadeo Torres the questions put to candidates at the time of their ordination (BCO 21-5), the minutes of the Missouri Presbytery’s Stated Meeting of 21 April 1998 clearly indicate that Rev. Torres signed his ministerial obligation which includes his written assent to all the ordination questions. Presbytery considered Rev. Torres’ subscription to the Ministerial Obligation as fulfilling the requirement of BCO 13-6. However, to avoid confusion in the future, Missouri Presbytery will require a verbal response from each ordained minister seeking admission to the presbytery.

description: Allowed an unlicensed man to preach in a PCA pulpit for more than a year. BCO 19-1,20-2,22-5,22-6.
responses: Our Candidates and Credentials Committee attempted to work with Westminster PC, Booneville, for about a year. At first the committee attempted to bring the retired Methodist minister that was filling the pulpit through the trials for ordination. When it was clear that he would not be able to pass the theological exams, they spoke with the
elders of this small church (about 25 members) about their options. All
of this took about a year and a half the matter has finally been resolved
this past month when the church voted to pullout of the PCA.
Adopted

description: the minutes of 1999 were submitted too late to be reviewed by the committee.
Churches not reporting to presbytery. 40-1,2,3.
responses: in the past two years all of our churches submitted their
sessional records for review (reported). In 1998 Nashville Presbytery
only met three times and not all the churches reported in that calendar
year. We will take greater care to have our churches adhere to a schedule
that meets the requirements of the BCO.

description: Standing rules on session records should comply with BCO 40-1,2,3.
responses: nashville presbytery cannot detect the lack of compliance in
our Standing Rules with BCO 40-1,2,3, nor has this been pointed out
before. the sections o fours rs which appear to be germane to the
general exception, together with our adopted list of minutes rules, are
excerpted below:
3-1 Book of Church Order. Chapter 13 of the Book of Church Order, THE PRESBYTERY, shall govern Presbytery in all matters to
which this chapter speaks.
CHAPTER TEN: SESSIONAL RECORDS
10-1 RULES: Sessions shall keep their records according to rules
adopted by the Presbytery, securing from the stated clerk a copy of these
rules inserting the list of rules on the inside of the front cover of their
Sessional Records Book. a copy of the sheet listing the items by which
the annual check of sessional records is made, shall also be inserted in
the inside of the front cover of each sessional records Book. Sessions
are to inform themselves about the contents of both of these papers and to
seek to observe them in keeping Sessional Records Books,
10-2 EXAMINATION Sessional records shall be presented for
examination according to the schedule set by the Stated Clerk.
comments of approval or criticism concerning each set of records shall
be noted on the correction sheet for the help of those preparing the minutes.
Guidelines for keeping Session Minutes consistent with the Book of Church Order and Rules of Assembly Operation.
1. All Minutes should be typed or written legibly.
2. All Minutes are to be kept in one volume for permanent record.
3. The name of the church is to appear on the cover of the volume.
4. Pages are to be numbered consecutively.
5. No blank pages are to be left between records of meetings.
6. The minutes of the annual congregational meeting and all other called meetings are to be included in the book.

7. Every meeting is to be recorded as to date, place, time, name of those present, absent, and excused. The minutes are to show that the meeting was opened and closed with prayer and each meeting should be recorded so as to show an accurate record of the proceedings. Only motions passed need to be recorded.
8. Minutes should show the purpose of the meeting stated or called. If called, the exact call of the meeting must be stated, with evidence that only business was conducted.
9. The names of the elders and viewpoints expressed by them need not be recorded.
10. All meetings are to be attested by the clerk and the moderator and the minutes of each meeting are to be approved at that meeting or at subsequent meeting as read or corrected.
11. Joint meetings of the Session and Deacons are to be recorded.
12. Congregational meetings of the Session and Deacons are to be recorded.
13. The minutes should show that a quorum was present at any given meeting.
14. Records are to be examined as to whether the proceedings have been correctly recorded in the light of the Book of Church Order and the above guide of the Nashville Presbytery.
15. A copy of the NP Standing Rules and a copy of this sheet of guidelines should be included with the minutes.

Please show us how our rules fail to comply. The Nashville Presbytery is currently studying and changing its structure. This process will result in changes to the Standing Rules. We will take care to adopt rules that fully comply with the BCO.

description: No record of ordinands being asked if their views had changed since licensure BCO 21-4
responses: Nashville Presbytery asks ordinands about changes in views as a regular part of their examination. The clerk has not recorded responses which reflect no change of views, but he will in the future.

responses: Nashville Presbytery will take greater care to see that all commission reports and minutes are included with the minutes.

responses: Presbytery acknowledges the error and will endeavor to guard
against it occurrence in the future.

description: No quorum for called meeting. No ruling elder present BCO 13-4.
responses: The exception was holding called meetings without the
presence of ruling elders. Our response to this is that it did not actually
happen. the clerk reported the names of the churches represented, which
were, in fact, represented by ruling elders. He simply neglected to record
their names, not realizing the oversight since the church was listed as being
represented. Presbytery regrets the omission.

description: No quorum for called meeting. No ruling elder present. BCO 13-4.
responses: The exception was holding called meetings without the
presence of ruling elders our response to this is that it did not actually
happen The omission is there, but so were the elders. The Clerk
reported the names of the churches represented, which were, in fact,
represented by ruling elders He simply neglected to record their names,
not realizing the oversight since the church was listed as being
represented. Presbytery regrets the omission.

description: Licentiate being examined for ordination was not examined in sacraments nor asked if his views have changed since licensure. BCO 21-4
responses: Presbytery has instructed its Credentials Committee to
prepare a checklist of those areas required by the Book of Church Order
and that this checklist be utilized for Presbytery’s examination of each
licentiate.

description: Presbytery erred in allowing a congregation to dissolve a pastoral relationship. BCO 13-9-c.
responses: presbytery respectfully suggests that a careful reading of the
section of the minutes will disclose that the dissolution was
accomplished by presbytery upon adoption of the appropriate presbytery
Committee report.

description: Covenant questions should be addressed to the session, not the congregation in assistant pastor installation. BCO 21-10 .
responses: Presbytery acknowledges the error, and in the future will
equip each ordination and/or installation with a checklist of procedures
to insure that the Book of Church Order requirements are met.

excommunicated by Presbytery and that the restoration was “through”
the session of park view church
